title: Ingot canonical claims submission batch
description: A batch of canonical claims for POST /v1/claims. Formalizes the wire format accepted by Api.ClaimJson.parse/2 — see docs/CLAIMS_CONTRACT.md. A batch validates whole: one invalid claim rejects everything with per-index reasons ({index, error}); nothing partial enters the log.

claim.description: One canonical claim, discriminated by kind. Unknown extra fields are ignored by the engine today (see open question 6 in the spec).

code.description: A code as a 'scheme:value' string, split on the FIRST colon; both halves non-empty. Known schemes canonicalize (GTIN family folds to a 14-digit zero-filled GTIN); unknown schemes pass through as opaque, non-bridging strings.

source.description: Source attribution: who asserts this claim (org id, feed name, system name). Free-form, non-empty; resolution ranks sources.

validFrom.description: Bitemporal valid-time: ISO 8601 date on which the fact became true in the world. Optional; defaults to recorded_at (the server-side submission date). Date precision only.

identityClaim.description: Node claim: a source's listing (ref) asserts a set of codes. The clustering input — identity-class codes bridge listings into one identity. Slot: (source, ref), last-wins.

ref.description: The source's OWN listing id — stable per source. A later identity claim with the same (source, ref) replaces the earlier code-set.

attributeClaim.description: Node claim: a fact about a coded thing. Never bridges; survivorship decides the winning value per (code, field). Slot: (source, code, field), last-wins.

field.description: Free-form field name; localized fields use 'field:locale' (e.g. 'name:fr').

mediaClaim.description: Node claim: an asset attached to a coded thing. Slot: (source, asset, target), last-wins.

asset.description: The asset id in the submitting DAM/source.

role.description: Optional; 'primary' or 'secondary'. Engine behavior today: any value other than 'primary' (or absence) means secondary.

groupingClaim.description: Edge claim: this code belonged to legacy product `product` — the continuity edge to a predecessor system; drives legacy-id assignment. Slot: (source, code), last-wins.

product.description: The legacy system's own product id.
